title: Chocolate crackle cakes
subtitle: No-bake chocolate and rice krispie cakes set in paper cases
excerpt: A children's party classic made by melting chocolate, butter, and golden syrup together, stirring in rice krispies until coated, and spooning into paper cases to set. No oven required. The cases can be set in a muffin tin or bun tin to hold their shape while the chocolate firms up. Ready in about 30 minutes including setting time.
sourceNotes: No-bake chocolate cereal cakes using rice-based cereals and melted chocolate are documented in British and American domestic cookery from the mid-twentieth century. The basic format of melted chocolate and golden syrup mixed with puffed rice cereal, set in paper cases, appears in British school-age cooking guides and domestic baking books from the 1950s, now in the public domain.

recipe.batchNotes: Double the recipe easily. One batch fills a 12-hole bun tin; double for 24.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keep in a cool room or refrigerator in an airtight container for up to 4 days. They soften if left at room temperature in warm weather.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: No oven. Melted on the hob and set at room temperature or in the refrigerator.

recipeTools.notes: For holding the paper cases in shape while the chocolate sets.

glossaryTerms.definition: Chocolate seizes (turns lumpy and grainy) if water or steam enters it during melting. For crackle cakes, melt the chocolate in a saucepan over very low heat with the butter and golden syrup, stirring constantly. Remove from the heat as soon as the chocolate is melted; overheating causes the fat to separate. Do not cover the pan as steam from the contents can condense and drop back into the chocolate. Add the rice krispies off the heat so they do not steam and go soft.

content.text: The only technique to get right is

without overheating it. Once melted, remove the pan from the heat before adding the rice krispies; adding them to the hot pan makes them go soft. Work quickly once combined and spoon into the cases before the chocolate begins to set.

Place paper cases in a 12-hole bun tin. Melt the chocolate, butter, and golden syrup together in a small saucepan over very low heat, stirring continuously. Remove from the heat as soon as everything is melted and smooth. Sift in the cocoa powder and icing sugar and stir until incorporated. Allow to cool for 2 minutes, then fold in the rice krispies until every piece is coated.

Working quickly before the mixture begins to set, spoon into the paper cases, filling each about two-thirds full. Press lightly to compact. Leave at room temperature for 30 minutes or in the refrigerator for 15 minutes until completely set.
